behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: yes—mocked diff records can hide real rename-detection edge cases. REAL-INFRA: yes—requires real git behavior (rename detection/similarity) and CLI execution. MOCK-HIDE: yes—if rename output is mocked, double-counting bugs may never appear. LLM-DEP: no. MULTI-STEP: no.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temporary real git repo, commit a file, rename it (`git mv`), commit again, run the CLI as a subprocess, and assert stdout report has the file only in `renamed` and not in `added` or `deleted`.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: yes—binary diff formatting/encoding behavior is easy to mis-model in mocks. REAL-INFRA: yes—needs real git diff/subprocess and filesystem binary content. MOCK-HIDE: yes—mocking decoded text can miss Unicode/byte handling crashes. LLM-DEP: no. MULTI-STEP: no.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real repo, commit a binary file (e.g., random bytes), modify or add another binary, run the CLI subprocess, and assert process exits successfully and report count includes binary-file changes.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: yes—real git on unborn HEAD returns specific failure modes not captured by simple mocks. REAL-INFRA: yes—requires real repository state and CLI/subprocess handling. MOCK-HIDE: yes—mocking success paths can mask exception handling gaps. LLM-DEP: no. MULTI-STEP: no.
behaviors.test_description: Create a new git repo with no commits, run the CLI subprocess against it, and assert zero exit status plus an empty structured report on stdout (not a stack trace/error).

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: yes—count mismatches often come from real git history traversal/details across commits. REAL-INFRA: yes—needs actual commit graph and CLI invocation. MOCK-HIDE: yes—precomputed mocked history can align with implementation and miss parsing/order bugs. LLM-DEP: no. MULTI-STEP: no.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history with known numbers of added/deleted/renamed/modified files across several commits, run the CLI subprocess, and compare emitted counts to the expected totals derived from the scripted history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: yes—staging/index vs working-tree semantics are git-native and easy to misrepresent in-process. REAL-INFRA: yes—requires real index/worktree state and subprocess execution. MOCK-HIDE: yes—mocked diff inputs can bypass staging-state bugs. LLM-DEP: no. MULTI-STEP: no.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, create equivalent change sets once as staged and once as unstaged (in separate controlled runs), execute the CLI subprocess for each, and assert stdout reports are identical.
